headlines.title: Car finance compensation details to be revealed with average payout of £700 expected
headlines.description: The City regulator will outline how millions of people can claim compensation for mis-sold car finance.

headlines.title: 'I dread the phone ringing': Inside the kennels responding to vicious XL bully attacks
headlines.description: Staff tell Panorama the aggression levels of the dangerous dogs they help to seize are rising.

headlines.title: Australian fugitive shot dead by police after seven months on the run
headlines.description: Dezi Freeman allegedly shot dead two police officers in the small Victorian town of Porepunkah in August.

headlines.title: Two hurt in Derby car incident released from hospital
headlines.description: Police have been given more time to question a man arrested on suspicion of attempted murder.

headlines.title: First stop, the Moon. Next stop, Mars? Why Nasa's mission matters
headlines.description: From a race with China to lunar discoveries, the US is investing time, effort and money to head to the Moon - and beyond.

headlines.title: Thieves steal Renoir, Cézanne and Matisse paintings in three-minute Italian heist
headlines.description: The paintings were taken from a museum in Parma by four masked men in the middle of the night, police say.

headlines.title: Councils in England urged to use universal parking app to cut 'unfair' fines
headlines.description: The National Parking Platform already has 15 councils on board with more in talks to join.

headlines.title: ‘Child-focused’ approach in family court battles is biggest change in 30 years, top judge says
headlines.description: Sir Andrew McFarlane welcomes the government backing for the change, saying it will turn the old approach "on its head".

headlines.title: Remove VAT from energy bills for three years, Tories urge
headlines.description: There has been a renewed focus on energy costs since the outbreak of the war in Iran.

headlines.title: Latin Patriarch will have access to Jerusalem holy site after police stopped entry
headlines.description: The Israeli PM said Cardinal Pierbattista Pizzaballa had been asked not to enter the church out of concern for his safety.

headlines.title: 75% of fans oppose VAR in Premier League, says Football Supporters' Association
headlines.description: Fans believe VAR is ruining goal celebrations and has not improved football since its introduction, according to a Football Supporters' Association survey.

headlines.title: Why does the US have Iran's Kharg Island in its sights?
headlines.description: The tiny island is home to one of the most critical pieces of Iran's energy infrastructure.

headlines.title: Fuel rations and free buses: How countries are responding to rising oil prices
headlines.description: Governments around the world have introduced measures to limit the impact of price increases.

headlines.title: Photos show heavily damaged US radar jet at Saudi base
headlines.description: US Central Command has not yet publicly commented on the incident.

headlines.title: Oil rises above $115 and Asia shares slide as Iran war enters fifth week
headlines.description: It comes after Iran-backed Houthi rebels in Yemen joined the conflict by striking Israel over the weekend.

headlines.title: Seven years since Emiliano Sala's death, what has changed for the 'wild west' of football transfers?
headlines.description: The Argentine striker’s 2019 death in a plane crash shone a light on the opaque world of transfers and player welfare.

headlines.title: Harry Potter star Paapa Essiedu hopes his TV drama Babies will break pregnancy loss silence
headlines.description: The BBC drama follows a couple in their 30s trying for a baby and navigating the complexities of pregnancy loss.

headlines.title: Tech CEOs suddenly love blaming AI for mass job cuts. Why?
headlines.description: More tech leaders are pointing to job cuts caused by AI tools - and a need for more investment cash.

headlines.title: The Papers:  'Horror on the street' and 'Fears grow over shortages'
headlines.description: Several of Monday's papers lead with images from Derby after a car struck pedestrians in the city centre.

headlines.title: How Australia’s seven-month-long manhunt came to an end
headlines.description: Fugitive Dezi Freeman has been wanted since he shot dead two police officers on his rural property in August last year.

headlines.title: How and when to see April's full Pink Moon
headlines.description: When will you be able to see April's full Moon and why is it called the Pink Moon?

headlines.title: I turned down MMA fighter on a night out - so he punched me
headlines.description: Anne Marie Boyle suffered a brain injury and subsequent seizures which led to her losing her business, her ability to drive and her confidence.

headlines.title: Partial government shutdown becomes the longest in US history
headlines.description: The 44-day funding lapse for the Department of Homeland Security has led to travel chaos at US airports.

headlines.title: Emotional Woodland seals first win since brain surgery
headlines.description: Having recovered from brain surgery and battled PTSD, an emotional Gary Woodland seals his first win in seven years at the Houston Open.

headlines.title: Bronze Age shield found in peat bog returned to Scotland for first time in over 230 years
headlines.description: The shield is one of six to feature in an exhibition on Bronze Age Scotland this summer.

headlines.title: BBC News app
headlines.description: Top stories, breaking news, live reporting, and follow news topics that match your interests

headlines.title: Why Josh Simons Resigned From Government
headlines.description: The former minister says he’d been ‘naive’ and is ‘sorry’ over journalist investigation.

headlines.title: What is RFK doing to vaccines for children in America?
headlines.description: Trump’s Health Secretary hits a major setback to “Make America Healthy Again”

headlines.title: Lively, bold animated Spider-Man adventure
headlines.description: Gaining powers like his hero Spider-Man puts teen Miles in danger, but he is not alone.

headlines.title: Spurs want De Zerbi to be new permanent boss
headlines.description: Tottenham Hotspur want to convince Roberto de Zerbi to become their new permanent head coach, after the departure of interim boss Igor Tudor.

headlines.title: Sinner raises Alcaraz stakes with historic 'Sunshine Double'
headlines.description: Jannik Sinner creating history by adding the Miami Open title to his Indian Wells win will not be lost on world number one Carlos Alcaraz.

headlines.title: Spurs have addressed their Tudor error - but cannot afford another
headlines.description: Chief executive Vinai Venkatesham and sporting director Johan Lange must get the next Tottenham appointment correct or else they could follow Igor Tudor, writes Phil McNulty.

headlines.title: 75% of fans oppose VAR in Premier League, says FSA
headlines.description: Fans believe VAR is ruining goal celebrations and has not improved football since its introduction, according to a Football Supporters' Association survey.

headlines.title: England players do not play enough county cricket - Lehmann
headlines.description: Northamptonshire head coach Darren Lehmann says England players do not play enough county cricket and selectors have overlooked talent in the domestic game.

headlines.title: Shades of Gascoigne - the rise of England's Anderson
headlines.description: Four years ago Elliot Anderson was helping Bristol Rovers to League Two promotion - this summer he could be a key player for England at the World Cup. BBC Sport charts his rise.
